task_completion.description: Verify Stellarium is set to the correct location (High Island) and time (May 1, 2024, 05:00 UTC).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom status bar or the location/date windows in Stellarium. The location should indicate High Island (or coordinates ~29.58° N, 94.38° W) and the time should be 2024-05-01 05:00.

task_completion.description: Verify the atmosphere and ground/landscape are disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a completely black sky background (no blue atmospheric scattering) and stars visible all the way to the bottom of the screen (no landscape, grass, or horizon blocking the view).

task_completion.description: Verify constellation lines, constellation boundaries, and the equatorial coordinate grid are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for stick-figure lines connecting stars (constellation lines), distinct boundary lines separating constellations (usually red or dashed), and a web of curved grid lines (equatorial grid) across the sky.

task_completion.description: Verify the view is centered on the star Polaris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Polaris selected (indicated by a target reticle and/or text info in the top left) and positioned in the exact center of the screen. The equatorial grid lines should converge at this central point.

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot was saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window, terminal output, or Stellarium on-screen notification confirming an image file was saved in the ~/Pictures/stellarium/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the research note contains the required observation details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal showing the contents of ~/Desktop/avian_navigation_notes.txt. The text must explicitly include the terms 'High Island', 'Polaris', 'May 1, 2024', and 'boundaries'.

integrity.description: Check that the agent interacted with the actual Stellarium GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the genuine Stellarium application interface being configured, not just a text editor modifying configuration files or a web browser looking up images.

integrity.description: Check that the saved screenshot is an actual capture of the agent's Stellarium session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot file shown in the file manager or image viewer should match the exact visual configuration (grid, boundaries, Polaris centered) seen in the Stellarium application window.

integrity.description: Check that the agent genuinely created the research note.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen typing or echoing the text into the file ~/Desktop/avian_navigation_notes.txt, rather than copying a pre-made file from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: High Island, Texas (29.5833° N, -94.3833° W, altitude 5m). Target Date/Time: 2024-05-01 05:00:00 UTC. Required Stellarium visual flags: Atmosphere OFF, Landscape OFF, Constellation Drawing ON, Constellation Boundaries ON, Equatorial Grid ON. Notes file path: /home/ga/Desktop/avian_navigation_notes.txt. Screenshots directory: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium.
